---
title: "RSV Immunisation Cost-Effectiveness Analysis — British Columbia"
subtitle: "Individual-level microsimulation with seven immunisation strategies"
author: "Javad Taleshi"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
output:
  html_document:
    toc: true
    toc_float: true
    theme: flatly
    df_print: kable
  pdf_document:
    toc: true
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = FALSE, warning = FALSE, message = FALSE,
                      fig.width = 8, fig.height = 5)
options(scipen = 999)
rm(list = ls())
```

```{r source-modules}
source("../R/01_packages.R")
source("../R/02_parameters.R")
source("../R/03_data_loading.R")
source("../R/04_vaccine_efficacy.R")
source("../R/05_cohort.R")
source("../R/06_strategies.R")
source("../R/07_model_functions.R")
source("../R/08_microsimulation.R")
source("../R/09_run_simulation.R")
source("../R/10_cea.R")
source("../R/11_sensitivity_analysis.R")

l_params              <- build_params(data_path = "../Data")
l_params              <- add_rsvma_vectors(l_params)
vaccine_efficacy_list <- build_vaccine_efficacy_list(l_params)
```

## Background

This report evaluates the cost-effectiveness of seven RSV immunisation strategies
for infants in British Columbia, Canada, using a 24-month individual-level
microsimulation model.

**Strategies compared**

| # | Strategy |
|---|----------|
| 1 | No Intervention |
| 2 | Palivizumab (historical standard of care) |
| 3 | Nirsevimab — high- and moderate-risk infants |
| 4 | RSVpreF maternal vaccine only |
| 5 | RSVpreF + nirsevimab (high-risk) |
| 6 | RSVpreF + nirsevimab (high- and moderate-risk) |
| 7 | Nirsevimab — all infants (universal) |

## Base-case results

```{r run-base, cache=TRUE}
results_table <- run_base_case(
  l_params              = l_params,
  vaccine_efficacy_list = vaccine_efficacy_list,
  n_i                   = n_i,
  n_sim                 = n_sim,
  seed                  = seed,
  output_dir            = "../Output"
)
```

```{r show-results}
results_table %>%
  select(Strategy, Total_Cost, Vaccine_Cost, Inpatient_Cost,
         ICU_Count, D_Count, Total_QALY, ICER) %>%
  mutate(across(c(Total_Cost, Vaccine_Cost, Inpatient_Cost),
                ~ scales::dollar(round(.x))),
         across(c(ICU_Count, D_Count), ~ round(.x, 1)),
         Total_QALY = round(Total_QALY, 1),
         ICER       = ifelse(is.na(ICER) | is.infinite(ICER), "—",
                             scales::dollar(round(ICER))))
```

## Cost-effectiveness frontier

```{r cea, cache=TRUE}
cea_out <- run_cea(results_table, output_dir = "../Output")
```

```{r show-cea-table}
cea_out$table_cea
```

```{r show-icer-plot, fig.cap="Cost-effectiveness plane. Dominated strategies are shown with dashed lines."}
cea_out$icer_plot
```

## One-way sensitivity analysis

```{r owsa, cache=TRUE}
owsa_nmb <- run_owsa(l_params, output_dir = "../Output", nsamp = 20, n_wtp = 50000)
```

```{r tornado, fig.cap="Tornado plot: expected net monetary benefit (WTP = $50,000/QALY). The bar length reflects the range of NMB as each parameter moves between its lower and upper bound."}
knitr::include_graphics("../Output/owsa_tornado.png")
```

```{r owsa-curves, fig.cap="OWSA sensitivity curves by strategy (excluding universal nirsevimab for clarity)."}
knitr::include_graphics("../Output/owsa_excl_universal.png")
```

## Two-way sensitivity analysis

```{r twsa, cache=TRUE}
twsa_nmb <- run_twsa(l_params, output_dir = "../Output", nsamp = 20, n_wtp = 50000)
```

```{r twsa-plot, fig.cap="Optimal strategy (highest NMB) across RSVpreF and nirsevimab price combinations at WTP = $50,000/QALY."}
knitr::include_graphics("../Output/twsa_nmb.png")
```

## Model parameters

Key parameters used in the base-case analysis:

```{r params-table}
data.frame(
  Parameter = c("Cohort size", "Follow-up", "Discount rate",
                "Nirsevimab price", "RSVpreF price", "Palivizumab (seasonal)",
                "ICU cost/day", "Ward cost/day",
                "Nirsevimab peak efficacy (RSVMA)", "RSVpreF peak efficacy (RSVMA)"),
  Value = c(
    format(n_i, big.mark = ","),
    "24 months",
    "0 %",
    paste0("$", l_params$c_nirsevimab),
    paste0("$", l_params$c_RSVpreF),
    paste0("$", l_params$c_palivizumab, "/season"),
    paste0("$", l_params$PD_ICU, "/day"),
    paste0("$", l_params$PD_PW, "/day"),
    paste0(round(l_params$efficacy_RSVMA_nirsevimab * 100), "%"),
    paste0(round(l_params$efficacy_RSVMA_RSVpreF    * 100), "%")
  )
)
```
